question.text: Under a Trading Plan, trading on behalf of the insider shall not commence earlier than how many calendar days from public disclosure of the plan?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Regulation 5(2)(i), the Trading Plan shall not entail commencement of trading earlier than one hundred and twenty calendar days from public disclosure. **The Trap:** 90 days may be confused with quarterly reporting cycles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The long cooling period ensures that any UPSI known at the time of formulation becomes stale by the time trading begins.
